objective: Propagate and document the dedicated SoM HITL SMS sender env keys from the governance vault into local-ai-machine bootstrap output.
scope_boundary: Modify the governance env bootstrap, environment registry, external services runbook, focused bootstrap contract test, issue #421 planning/validation/closeout artifacts, and graph/wiki artifacts if generated by repo hooks.
Use placeholder values in tests and redacted dry-run output only.
Do not commit .env.shared, generated repo env files, raw phone numbers, Twilio credentials, webhook payloads, or Twilio dashboard evidence.
out_of_scope: Provisioning or purchasing a Twilio number.
Using the AIS/Alex or customer-demo sender as the production SoM approval route.
research_summary: Issue #416 already made local-ai-machine bootstrap consume the gitignored governance vault safely and emit operator notification keys. The remaining gap is the dedicated SoM HITL sender key and runbook guidance that keeps approval replies isolated from AIS/customer-demo Twilio paths.

sprints.name: Dedicated SoM HITL sender SSOT
sprints.goal: Make the governance bootstrap and runbooks explicitly carry the dedicated SoM Twilio sender without exposing phone numbers or secrets.
tasks: Add SoM sender env names to the local-ai-machine bootstrap output.
Extend the bootstrap contract test for required key presence and dry-run redaction.
Update the environment registry and external services runbook with sender separation rules.
Run focused and local-ci validation, then open a draft PR.
acceptance_criteria: The local-ai-machine bootstrap output contains SOM_TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER, TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER, and TWILIO_SMS_FROM.
Dry-run validation redacts sender values and does not leak placeholder phone numbers.
Docs identify SOM_TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER as the production SoM HITL approval sender.
Docs state the AIS/Alex and customer-demo senders are not production SoM approval routes.
No secrets, raw phone numbers, .env.shared contents, generated .env files, or Twilio webhook payloads are committed.
Final AC: Local CI and GitHub PR checks pass.

specialist_reviews.focus: Preferred specialist model for implementation review.
specialist_reviews.summary: gpt-5.3-codex-spark quota was unavailable before work began; fallback review used a read-only lighter Codex specialist.

specialist_reviews.focus: Required artifacts and validation commands for the governance surface change.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed the slice needs a dedicated issue, progress/backlog mirrors, plan/scope artifacts, focused tests, validation evidence, closeout, and Local CI.

alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted the governance artifact and validation checklist for this mechanical bootstrap/docs slice.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #421 may update governance env bootstrap, docs, focused tests, issue planning/validation/closeout artifacts, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate locally, open PR, merge after checks pass, and close issue #421.

material_deviation_rules: If validation output includes raw phone numbers or Twilio secrets, delete the evidence and rerun with redaction.
If a new Twilio number or A2P campaign is required, stop before purchasing or dashboard mutation and obtain explicit operator approval.
If downstream local-ai-machine runtime changes are required, create a separate downstream issue/lane.
